calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jul-11-2019 Off Calendar

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Apr-23-2019 Continued To Jul-23-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610
MINI MINUTES: Special Appearance by Vivian Lerche, Esq. for Plaintiff(s). The Order to Show Cause is re-set Jul-23-2019 at 10:30 AM in Dept. 610. JUDGE GARRET L. WONG; Clerk JLee. Proceedings not reported. (610)

COURT TRIAL
Off Calendar Jan-22-2019 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Apr-23-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar this day, having been assigned by Dept. 501 in order to place the terms of the parties' settlement agreement on the record.
Judge: Gail Dekreon. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty. Reporter: Mitch L. Lyon, CSR #6048. Telephone: 415-551-3703.

Jeffery Mukai, Esq. appeared for plaintiff Wells Fargo Bank, N.A.
JayCee C. VanSickle, Loan Adjuster for plaintiff, was placed under oath and testified.
Defendant Melody L. Belocura was present in pro per, placed under oath and testified.

The terms of the agreement, which were read into the record by Mr. Mukai, are as follows:

(1) Defendant Melody L. Belocura shall pay plaintiff Wells Fargo Bank, N.A. the sum of $4,233.00 in two installments. Defendant shall pay defendant $2,116.50 on or before February 28, 2019, and shall pay the balance of $2,116.50 on or before March 28, 2019;

(2) The Court shall retain jurisdiction over this matter pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure Section 664.46; and

(3) In the event defendant Melody L. Belocura fails to comply with the terms of this agreement, plaintiff shall provide defendant with seven days to cure the breach. If defendant fails to cure the breach, plaintiff may, upon ex parte application with forty-eight notice to defendant, obtain judgment in the amount of $5,644.63, plus Court costs, less any payments defendant has made prior to the breach.

Additionally, the parties waive the terms and conditions of Civil Code Section 1542 which reads as follows:

A general release does not extend to claims which the creditor does not know or suspect to exist in his or her favor at the time of executing the release, which if known by him or her must have materially affected his or her settlement with the debtor.

Defendant Melody L. Belocura was placed under oath and voir dired by the Court as to the terms of the agreement, and her understanding therein.

Matter is CONTINUED to April 23, 2019 at 10:30 a.m. in Department 610 on the CRC 3.1385 Dismissal Calendar.

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Jan-22-2019 Assigned To Trial Court
